title: CEX-Gateway Hybrid Arbitrage Feasibility

concept.description: Use CEX (Binance/Bybit) as price oracle + hedge, Gateway as execution venue
flow: 1. Monitor CEX BTC price feed (websocket, <100ms latency)
2. When CEX price diverges from Gateway quote by >threshold:
3a. If CEX BTC dropped: Sell BTC via Gateway at stale high price
3b. If CEX BTC pumped: Buy BTC via Gateway at stale low price
4. Simultaneously hedge on CEX (buy/sell spot or futures)
5. Close hedge after Gateway settlement (6-9 min)

advantages: CEX gives sub-second price discovery — much faster than CoinGecko 30s cache
CEX hedge eliminates directional risk during 6-9 min Gateway settlement
CEX spot fees: 0.10% (Binance VIP0) vs Gateway 0.42% — but Gateway is the lagging side
No need to time the market — just monitor the spread
problems: CEX requires KYC and separate capital allocation (not ring-fenced)
CEX withdrawal to fund Gateway takes 30-60 min (not instant)
Hedging on futures requires margin — exposes to liquidation risk
Our $300 budget cannot be split between CEX+Gateway effectively
BTC withdrawal from CEX costs ~$7.16 — kills small trades
AGENTS.md prohibits leverage, perps — futures hedge is not allowed
LLM cannot be in execution decision path — but CEX API integration needed
blockers: AGENTS.md: No leverage, no perps — eliminates futures hedge
AGENTS.md: No unlimited approvals — limits CEX integration
$300 budget: cannot split between CEX ($150 min) + Gateway ($150)
BTC withdrawal fee ($7.16) makes sub-$500 trades unviable
No CEX account or API keys provisioned

verdict.reason: Project rules (no perps, no leverage) and budget constraints ($300) make CEX-Gateway hybrid unviable. BTC withdrawal fee alone ($7.16) is 2.4% of a $300 position. Without futures hedge, directional risk during 6-9 min settlement is too high.
